subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Two-layer guard (structural mint check for DECLARED warrants + discovery process layer for UNDECLARED capability-presuming ACs) is correct and the honest boundary is sound. Defect premise verified against source: adv_change_archive has no target_path, so the originating AC6 assertion was genuinely unwarranted (KD5 backfill correctly scopes to status-repair routing). KD2 import-cycle premise is real (contract.ts->contract-mint; tool-registry->contractTools), and a generated no-runtime-import manifest is a valid break. However it is the heaviest of three cycle-free options: (a) extend existing parity-guarded ADV_TOOL_NAMES to carry arg-key sets; (b) inject live toolSurface from the impure tool wrapper (which already imports the registry) into the existing KD3 resolveWarrant seam, removing the generated artifact and new check entirely. Spec-law composition is clean: discovery owns AC firming, mint already runs post-approval, new rq-* are additive, and C5 aligns with P33.

validation.notes: VALIDATED on gap-closure (dim 1) and spec-law (dim 3). CAUTION concentrated on KD2 mechanism choice: the generated tool-surface manifest + new tool-surface:check is heavier than needed. Evaluate injecting live toolSurface arg-keys from the tool layer (tools/contract.ts already imports the registry) into the pure buildContractFromAgreement via the existing KD3 resolveWarrant seam before committing to a generated artifact. If a static artifact is preferred for determinism, model the gate exactly on schemas:check and prefer extending ADV_TOOL_NAMES over a new generated file. Manifest staleness risk: warrants verify against the artifact, so the drift check must be wired into pnpm run check or false pass/fail can occur on arg renames.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Proceed; no blocker. Record the KD2 source-of-truth decision explicitly. Preferred order: (1) inject live toolSurface from the impure adv_contract_mint wrapper into pure buildContractFromAgreement via the existing resolveWarrant seam (no new build artifact, no new check); (2) if a static artifact is required, extend the existing parity-guarded ADV_TOOL_NAMES structure and model the gate on schemas:check wired into pnpm run check. Keep KD1/KD3/KD4/KD5 as designed.
follow_ups: Decide and document KD2 source-of-truth: injected live toolSurface (lighter) vs generated manifest vs ADV_TOOL_NAMES extension.
If generated manifest chosen, wire tool-surface:check into pnpm run check and add a parity/drift test mirroring tool-registry.test.ts:66 to prevent stale-surface false verdicts.

wisdom_candidates.content: Capability-presuming acceptance criteria can be structurally checked without heuristic prose inference by requiring explicit `[warrant: ...]` tags during discovery, then verifying declared refs against live tool/spec surfaces at contract mint.

wisdom.content: A failed reproduction attempt is NOT a requirement. In discovery, classify every reproduction-sourced finding as broken_capability | unwarranted_operation | unverified before it can seed an acceptance criterion; unwarranted_operation/unverified findings must never seed a "must-work" criterion. Never harden a hedged/unverified observation into a firm AC. Capability-presuming criteria (those asserting a tool/arg/spec surface exists or must work) must declare a `[warrant: tool:<name>#<arg> | spec:<rq-id>]` tag, verified structurally against the live tool surface at contract mint (CONTRACT_UNRESOLVED_WARRANT fail-fast). Behavioral criteria need no warrant (proportionality). Real defect that motivated this: fixStaleCloseVisibility AC6 asserted adv_change_archive target_path routing — archive has no target_path and shouldn't (phase9 git finalization is repo-local). Caught only by the late design validator; the guard moves the catch to discovery + mint. Source-of-truth for "does this surface exist": tool-registry getToolSurface() injected via runtime dynamic import to keep the pure validator cycle-free.

items.text: **SC1:** An acceptance criterion that presumes a capability surface which does not exist cannot reach planning — it is rejected at or before contract mint with a precise, actionable error.
**SC2:** A failed reproduction attempt is explicitly distinguishable from a real requirement in discovery output, so it cannot silently seed a criterion asserting the capability must work.
**SC3:** Changes with no capability-presuming criteria gain no added ceremony (proportionality preserved).

**AC1:** Given an agreement that declares a capability warrant naming a tool surface absent from the tool registry, when the contract is minted, then mint fails fast with a precise error identifying the unresolved warrant.
**AC2:** Given an agreement that declares a capability warrant naming a real tool surface, when the contract is minted, then mint succeeds.
**AC3:** Given an agreement whose criteria declare no capability warrants (purely behavioral), when the contract is minted, then mint succeeds unchanged.

**AC4:** The `adv-discover` command contract requires every reproduction-sourced finding to be classified as broken_capability | unwarranted_operation | unverified, and forbids an unwarranted_operation or unverified finding from seeding a criterion asserting the capability must work.
**AC5:** The `advance-workflow` spec contains a normative requirement (rq-*) for capability-warrant validation at contract mint and reproduction-finding classification at discovery.
**AC6:** `fixStaleCloseVisibility` AC6 is corrected to reflect status-repair cross-project routing only, with archive's no-target_path-by-design rationale recorded.
**AC7:** `pnpm run check` and the affected plugin test suites pass.
**C1:** Patch source in `/home/jon/dev/advance`; no deployed-artifact edits.
**C2:** The primary guard is structural (registry/spec verification at mint); prose rules are secondary, never the sole authority (P33).
**C3:** The warrant requirement is proportional — only capability-presuming criteria carry warrants.
**C4:** Existing specs are law; extend without contradiction. Existing agreements without declared warrants must continue to mint.
**C5:** No heuristic NLP inference of "presumed capability" from free prose as the verification authority.
**DONT1:** Do not treat a failed reproduction attempt as a requirement.
**DONT2:** Do not allow a hedged/unverified observation to harden into a firm criterion without a declared warrant.
**DONT3:** Do not rely solely on the design-phase validator as the catch for unwarranted criteria.
**DONT4:** Do not impose warrant ceremony on behavioral criteria that presume no capability surface.

Proportional: only capability-presuming criteria carry warrants; behavioral untouched (AC3).
Existing warrant-less agreements still mint (AC3); new rq-acWarrant01 additive, no spec contradiction.
No heuristic NLP inference of presumed capability; only declared warrants verified.
Failed repro != requirement: classification rule + warrant-declaration enforce this.
Hedge not hardened: unverified classification blocks seeding must-work criteria.
Design validator not sole catch: mint-time structural check + discovery classification layers added.
No warrant ceremony on behavioral criteria (C3 + AC3 proportionality test).

acceptanceCriteria: **AC1:** Given an agreement that declares a capability warrant naming a tool surface absent from the tool registry, when the contract is minted, then mint fails fast with a precise error identifying the unresolved warrant.
**AC2:** Given an agreement that declares a capability warrant naming a real tool surface, when the contract is minted, then mint succeeds.
**AC3:** Given an agreement whose criteria declare no capability warrants (purely behavioral), when the contract is minted, then mint succeeds unchanged.

**AC4:** The `adv-discover` command contract requires every reproduction-sourced finding to be classified as broken_capability | unwarranted_operation | unverified, and forbids an unwarranted_operation or unverified finding from seeding a criterion asserting the capability must work.
**AC5:** The `advance-workflow` spec contains a normative requirement (rq-*) for capability-warrant validation at contract mint and reproduction-finding classification at discovery.
**AC6:** `fixStaleCloseVisibility` AC6 is corrected to reflect status-repair cross-project routing only, with archive's no-target_path-by-design rationale recorded.
**AC7:** `pnpm run check` and the affected plugin test suites pass.

documents.proposal:
## Problem

ADV's discovery → agreement flow can produce acceptance criteria founded on a false premise — a presumed capability that does not exist or is not architecturally warranted. This happened concretely in change `fixStaleCloseVisibility` (AC6 asserted "target_path archive routing" when `adv_change_archive` has no `target_path` surface and architecturally should not). The criterion was only caught by the design-phase validator, after it had already been minted into the contract.

Root causes observed:
- A failed reproduction attempt was treated as a confirmed capability gap, without classifying "broken capability that should work" vs "user attempted an unwarranted operation."
- A hedged discovery observation ("needs verification end-to-end") was hardened into a firm acceptance criterion.
- Two non-symmetric operations (status-repair vs archive) were bundled into one criterion.

## Why This Matters

The catch happened at the wrong gate. The design validator is a backstop, not a gate-zero guard. Any change whose discovery makes the same mistake can mint an unwarranted criterion into its contract; if its design validator misses it (or the change is small/fast), the unwarranted criterion drives planning and execution work that should never have existed. This is a systemic discovery-quality defect, not a one-off.

## User Outcomes

- Reproduction-sourced findings are explicitly classified before they can seed an acceptance criterion (broken-capability vs unwarranted-operation vs unverified).
- Every acceptance criterion that presumes a tool/capability surface declares that surface and is checked against reality (tool registry / spec / code) or carries an explicit warrant rationale.
- Hedged/unverified observations cannot be silently hardened into firm criteria.
- The guard fires at discovery (earliest gate), not only at the design validator.
- Prefer a structural mechanism (typed warrant + machine check against the registry) over prose-only rules, per P33.

## Scope

### In Scope

- Discovery command contract (`.opencode/command/adv-discover.md`): add a reproduction-finding classification step and an acceptance-criteria "warrant check" at the AC checkpoint.
- `advance-workflow` spec (`.adv/specs/`): add the normative requirement(s) for finding-classification and AC warrant validation.
- A structural enforcement path: where feasible, ACs that name a tool/capability surface are machine-checkable against the tool registry / schema / spec, so an AC presuming a non-existent surface fails fast rather than relying on agent reasoning.
- Asset/unit tests that enforce the command contract and the structural check.
- Backfill: apply the corrected classification to `fixStaleCloseVisibility` AC6 (or coordinate its discovery re-entry) so the originating defect is closed, not just guarded against going forward.
- A durable wisdom entry capturing the rule.

### Out of Scope

- Rewriting the broader discovery protocol beyond the classification + warrant additions.
- Changing the design-phase validator (it stays as the backstop; this change adds the earlier guard).
- The runtime terminal-state fix in `fixStaleCloseVisibility` (separate change; only its AC6 criterion is touched here as backfill).
- Any heuristic NLP that infers "presumed capability" from free prose as the sole authority (must be structural/declared, per P33).

### Must Not

- Must not rely solely on prose rules the agent may ignore; the primary guard should be structural where the surface allows.
- Must not treat a failed reproduction attempt as a requirement.
- Must not allow a hedged/unverified discovery observation to become a firm AC without an explicit warrant.

## Affected Surfaces (discovery will confirm)

- `.opencode/command/adv-discover.md` — Phase 1.8 completeness verification, Phase 2 ambiguity analysis, Phase 4.5.1 AC checkpoint.
- `.adv/specs/advance-workflow/spec.json` (+ markdown mirror if present) — new `rq-*` requirement(s).
- `plugin/src/tools/contract.ts` / contract-mint validation — candidate site for a structural warrant check against the tool registry.
- `plugin/src/tool-registry.ts` / tool schemas — source of truth for "does this surface exist."
- Asset tests under `plugin/src/**/*-assets.test.ts` and command/spec anchor tests.

## Constraints

- Patch source in `/home/jon/dev/advance`; no deployed-artifact edits.
- Prefer structural correctness (typed warrant + registry check) over heuristic prose (P33).
- Keep the guard proportional — narrow/local changes with no reproduction-sourced capability claims must not be burdened.
- Existing specs are law; extend, do not contradict.

## Impact

Critical for discovery quality. Closing this gap prevents unwarranted criteria from entering contracts across all future changes, moving the catch from a late, fallible backstop to an early structural gate.

## Discovery Agenda

- Decide the precise structural mechanism: typed `capability_warrant` field on reproduction-derived ACs verified against the tool registry/spec, vs a contract-mint-time check, vs an AC-checkpoint checklist with a machine-checkable subset.
- Determine how to classify reproduction findings without over-burdening narrow changes (proportionality).
- Confirm where the check is cheapest and hardest to bypass (discovery output schema, contract mint, or both).
- Define the backfill path for `fixStaleCloseVisibility` AC6.
documents.problemStatement: ADV discovery can harden a hedged/unverified observation into a firm acceptance criterion that presumes a capability which does not exist or is not architecturally warranted. A failed reproduction attempt gets treated as a confirmed requirement, and non-symmetric operations get bundled into one criterion. Today the only thing that catches this is the design-phase validator (a late backstop, after the criterion is already minted into the contract). There is no discovery-phase guard that classifies reproduction-sourced findings (broken-capability vs unwarranted-operation vs unverified) or that validates each acceptance criterion's presumed capability/surface actually exists or is justified. The implementation-free guard checks the inverse (too much mechanism), not unwarranted premise. Result: unwarranted criteria can reach planning/execution in other changes.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement

## Objectives

1. Move the unwarranted-criterion catch from the late design-phase validator to an early, structural gate: a machine-verified capability-warrant check at contract mint.
2. Force classification of reproduction-sourced discovery findings (broken_capability | unwarranted_operation | unverified) so a failed attempt cannot silently become a requirement.
3. Add a declared-warrant mechanism for capability-presuming criteria, verified against the tool registry / spec source of truth.
4. Encode the rule in the `advance-workflow` spec and the `adv-discover` command contract.
5. Backfill: correct `fixStaleCloseVisibility` AC6.
6. Capture a durable wisdom entry encoding the rule.

## Success Criteria

1. **SC1:** An acceptance criterion that presumes a capability surface which does not exist cannot reach planning — it is rejected at or before contract mint with a precise, actionable error.
2. **SC2:** A failed reproduction attempt is explicitly distinguishable from a real requirement in discovery output, so it cannot silently seed a criterion asserting the capability must work.
3. **SC3:** Changes with no capability-presuming criteria gain no added ceremony (proportionality preserved).

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **AC1:** Given an agreement that declares a capability warrant naming a tool surface absent from the tool registry, when the contract is minted, then mint fails fast with a precise error identifying the unresolved warrant.
2. **AC2:** Given an agreement that declares a capability warrant naming a real tool surface, when the contract is minted, then mint succeeds.
3. **AC3:** Given an agreement whose criteria declare no capability warrants (purely behavioral), when the contract is minted, then mint succeeds unchanged.
4. **AC4:** The `adv-discover` command contract requires every reproduction-sourced finding to be classified as broken_capability | unwarranted_operation | unverified, and forbids an unwarranted_operation or unverified finding from seeding a criterion asserting the capability must work.
5. **AC5:** The `advance-workflow` spec contains a normative requirement (rq-*) for capability-warrant validation at contract mint and reproduction-finding classification at discovery.
6. **AC6:** `fixStaleCloseVisibility` AC6 is corrected to reflect status-repair cross-project routing only, with archive's no-target_path-by-design rationale recorded.
7. **AC7:** `pnpm run check` and the affected plugin test suites pass.

## Constraints

1. **C1:** Patch source in `/home/jon/dev/advance`; no deployed-artifact edits.
2. **C2:** The primary guard is structural (registry/spec verification at mint); prose rules are secondary, never the sole authority (P33).
3. **C3:** The warrant requirement is proportional — only capability-presuming criteria carry warrants.
4. **C4:** Existing specs are law; extend without contradiction. Existing agreements without declared warrants must continue to mint.
5. **C5:** No heuristic NLP inference of "presumed capability" from free prose as the verification authority.

## Avoidances

1. **DONT1:** Do not treat a failed reproduction attempt as a requirement.
2. **DONT2:** Do not allow a hedged/unverified observation to harden into a firm criterion without a declared warrant.
3. **DONT3:** Do not rely solely on the design-phase validator as the catch for unwarranted criteria.
4. **DONT4:** Do not impose warrant ceremony on behavioral criteria that presume no capability surface.

## Preview Applicability

visual_surface: false — Change affects ADV discovery/command contract, contract-mint validator, spec, and tests only. No front-end or browser-visible output.

## Decisions

### User Decisions

1. **Warrant scope → only capability-presuming ACs.** Behavioral criteria need no warrant; only criteria claiming a tool/capability surface must work require a declared, verified warrant. (User selected recommended option.)
2. **Enforcement → hard-block at contract mint.** Mint fails fast with a precise error when a declared warrant names a non-existent surface — the hardest-to-bypass point. (User selected recommended option.)
3. **Backfill → fix `fixStaleCloseVisibility` AC6 within this change.** Correct the originating defect here, not via separate later re-entry. (User selected recommended option.)

### Agent Decisions (LBP)

1. **Reuse existing mint fail-fast pattern.** Mint-time warrant verification follows the established `CONTRACT_DUPLICATE_ID` / `CONTRACT_ITEMS_EMPTY` throw pattern in `plugin/src/validator/contract-mint.ts` → new `CONTRACT_UNRESOLVED_WARRANT` error class.
2. **Declared warrant lives in the agreement artifact** so `contract-mint` can parse it deterministically; exact declaration syntax decided in design.
3. **Tool registry is the surface source of truth**; spec-requirement warrants verified against `.adv/specs`.
4. **Discovery classification enforced via command contract + asset test** (prose where structure can't reach), with the mint-time check as the hard structural backstop — addressing the "should-have-declared-but-didn't" gap.

## Deferred Questions

None.

## Sign-Off

Criteria and agreement approved by user inline ("approve"). Discovery → design.
documents.design:
# Design

> **Validator: CAUTION → resolved.** KD1/KD3/KD4/KD5 validated sound. KD2 revised per validator recommendation (below): live injected tool-surface via runtime dynamic import instead of a generated manifest + new check gate — simpler, zero drift risk, same objectives. No user-value tradeoff; auto-adopted.

## Architecture Overview

Two-layer guard with an honest division of authority:

- **Structural layer (hard, machine-verified):** `contract-mint` verifies every *declared* capability warrant against a live tool-surface lookup (and spec ids). A declared warrant naming a non-existent tool/arg/spec surface → `CONTRACT_UNRESOLVED_WARRANT` fail-fast, matching the existing `CONTRACT_DUPLICATE_ID` / `CONTRACT_ITEMS_EMPTY` pattern. (AC1/AC2/AC3)
- **Process layer (forces declaration):** the `adv-discover` command contract requires reproduction-sourced findings to be classified `broken_capability | unwarranted_operation | unverified`, forbids `unwarranted_operation`/`unverified` findings from seeding a must-work criterion, and requires capability-presuming criteria to declare a warrant. Enforced by an asset test + spec `rq-*`. (AC4/AC5)

**Honest boundary (P33, no overselling):** structure verifies *declared* warrants. It cannot detect an *undeclared* capability-presuming AC from free prose without heuristic NLP (forbidden by C5/DONT4). The process layer forces the declaration; the mint check makes a declared warrant truthful. The AC6 defect is caught by **both** paths: classified `unwarranted_operation` at discovery (blocked by AC4), and — if someone instead declared `tool:adv_change_archive#target_path` — rejected at mint because that arg does not exist (AC1).

## Key Decisions

### KD1 — Warrant declaration syntax in the agreement (parseable, proportional)
Capability-presuming criteria carry an inline warrant tag appended to the bullet:

```
- AC2: Cross-project status repair routes through the target namespace. [warrant: tool:adv_change_status_repair#target_path]
```

Grammar:
- `[warrant: <ref>(, <ref>)*]` — one tag per criterion, comma-separated refs.
- ref forms: `tool:<name>` (tool exists), `tool:<name>#<arg>` (tool arg exists), `spec:<rq-id>` (spec requirement exists).
- Behavioral criteria omit the tag entirely (C3/DONT4 — no ceremony).

`contract-mint` extracts the tag during `parseObligationLine`, strips it from the persisted `text`, and records refs on the contract item for verification. Unknown tag body or malformed ref → fail-fast (typed error), never silently ignored.

### KD2 (REVISED) — Live injected tool-surface, no generated artifact
`contract-mint.ts` currently imports only `../types`; a static import of the tool registry would create a cycle (`contractTools → tool-registry → contract-mint`). **Revised mechanism (validator-recommended):**

- `buildContractFromAgreement` stays **pure** and accepts an injected resolver: `resolveWarrant(ref) => boolean` (or a `{ toolSurface: Map<string, Set<string>>, specIds: Set<string> }` lookup object). No fs, no registry import (DDC2/DDC3).
- The tool-layer `adv_contract_mint` (`plugin/src/tools/contract.ts`) builds the live lookup at runtime via **dynamic `await import("../tool-registry")`** (cycle-free at runtime — same pattern as `change.ts` `await import("./_recovery-writers")`), exposing `getToolSurface()` that reads arg-key sets from the already-assembled tool map. Spec ids come from the store/specs already reachable in the tool layer.
- No generated `tool-surface.json`, no new `tool-surface:check` gate, **no drift risk** — the surface is always live/current.

Rejected (was original KD2): a generated manifest + drift check — heavier, adds a CI gate, and introduces a staleness surface the live approach avoids entirely.

### KD3 — Spec-ref warrants verified against `.adv/specs`
`spec:<rq-id>` refs verified by checking the requirement id exists in loaded specs. Injected into the pure validator alongside the tool-surface (single `resolveWarrant`/lookup seam). Unit tests inject fakes; one integration test exercises the real wiring.

### KD4 — Discovery classification + command contract (AC4/AC5)
`adv-discover.md`: extend Phase 1.8 / Phase 2 / Phase 4.5.1 with:
- A **Reproduction Finding Classification** requirement: each reproduction-sourced finding tagged `broken_capability | unwarranted_operation | unverified`.
- A rule: `unwarranted_operation`/`unverified` findings MUST NOT seed a criterion asserting the capability must work; they become out-of-scope notes or are downgraded.
- A **Warrant Declaration** step at the AC checkpoint: capability-presuming criteria must carry a `[warrant: ...]` tag.
Add `advance-workflow` `rq-*` requirement(s) as spec law; enforce command/spec presence via an asset/anchor test.

### KD5 — Backfill `fixStaleCloseVisibility` AC6
Correct that change's agreement AC6 to: *"Cross-project status-repair routes through the target namespace; `adv_change_archive` does not accept `target_path` by design (git finalization runs in the target repo)."* Re-mint its contract (paused at design — in-place agreement correction + re-mint). Rationale references this guard change.

## ADR Drafts

Candidate ADR (rubric met: hard-to-reverse syntax/authority choice + cross-cutting + real tradeoff): `docs/adr/NNNN-capability-warrant-declaration.md` — records declared-vs-inferred warrant decision and the live-injected-surface source of truth. Advisory; author during execution if time allows.

## Implementation Strategy

TDD red→green, sequenced (worktree-isolated):

1. **Warrant types + pure parse/verify (KD1/KD2/KD3)** — add optional `warrants?: string[]` to `ContractItemSchema`; extend `contract-mint` to parse/strip `[warrant: ...]` and call injected resolver; add `validator/warrant.ts` pure resolver. RED: AC1 (bad ref → `CONTRACT_UNRESOLVED_WARRANT`), AC2 (good ref → ok), AC3 (no tag → unchanged mint).
2. **Live wiring (KD2/KD3)** — `getToolSurface()` in tool-registry; `adv_contract_mint` dynamic-imports it + gathers specIds; injects into mint. RED: integration test mints against the real tool surface; boundary test asserts no static `tools/*`/`tool-registry` import from `contract-mint.ts`/`warrant.ts` (DDC3).
3. **Command + spec (KD4)** — edit `adv-discover.md`; add `advance-workflow` rq-*; asset/anchor tests (AC4/AC5).
4. **Backfill (KD5)** — correct `fixStaleCloseVisibility` AC6 + re-mint; verify (AC6).
5. **Wisdom + full gate** — wisdom entry; `pnpm run check` + affected suites (AC7).

## LBP Analysis

Declared-and-verified warrants (not inferred) is the only P33-compliant option — prose inference would be the exact heuristic-authority anti-pattern this change removes. The revised live-injected surface (vs. a generated manifest) reuses the established runtime dynamic-import pattern, keeps the validator pure, and eliminates a staleness surface and a CI gate. Rejected: (a) static registry import in validator (cycle); (b) generated manifest + drift check (heavier, staleness); (c) heuristic capability-language detection at mint (violates C5/DONT4); (d) validator-only catch (status quo — too late).

## Affected Components

- `plugin/src/validator/contract-mint.ts` — warrant tag parse + strip; call injected resolver (stays pure).
- `plugin/src/validator/warrant.ts` (new) — pure ref resolver over injected `{ toolSurface, specIds }`.
- `plugin/src/tools/contract.ts` — runtime dynamic-import `getToolSurface()`, gather specIds, inject into mint.
- `plugin/src/tool-registry.ts` — export `getToolSurface()` (name → arg-key set from the assembled tool map).
- `plugin/src/types/changes.ts` — optional `warrants?: string[]` on `ContractItemSchema`.
- `.opencode/command/adv-discover.md` — classification + warrant declaration steps.
- `.adv/specs/advance-workflow/spec.json` (+ md mirror) — new `rq-*`.
- Tests: `contract-mint.test.ts`, new `warrant.test.ts`, contract-mint↔registry integration test, import-boundary test, discover/spec asset tests.
- Backfill: `fixStaleCloseVisibility` agreement + contract.

No new `package.json` script / check gate (revised KD2).

## Design-Derived Criteria

- **DDC1 (purity):** `validator/warrant.ts` and `buildContractFromAgreement` stay pure — no fs/registry/tools import; all surface data injected.
- **DDC2 (no-cycle):** `contract-mint.ts` / `warrant.ts` must not statically import `tool-registry.ts` or any `tools/*` module; tool-layer wiring uses runtime dynamic import. Guarded by an import-boundary test.
- **DDC3 (liveness):** the tool-surface used for verification is read from the live assembled tool map at mint time — no cached/generated copy.

## Risks / Mitigations

- **R1: Dynamic import resolves the wrong/empty surface at runtime.** Mitigation: integration test mints against the real `getToolSurface()` and asserts a known real arg (e.g. `adv_change_status_repair#target_path`) resolves and a known-absent one (`adv_change_archive#target_path`) fails.
- **R2: Declaration burden frustrates authors.** Mitigation: proportional — only capability-presuming criteria need tags (AC3 proportionality test).
- **R3: Undeclared capability-presuming AC slips past mint.** Acknowledged boundary — caught by discovery classification (AC4), not mint. Documented honestly; not oversold.
- **R4: Import-cycle regression if someone later adds a static import.** Mitigation: DDC2 import-boundary test fails the build.
- **R5: Live behavior needs rebuild+restart** (source-vs-dist). Mitigation: verify via unit/integration tests in-session; note deferred live validation in archive notes.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary — addAcWarrantGuard

## Outcome

ADV discovery could harden a hedged or unverified observation into a firm acceptance criterion that presumed a capability which does not exist or is not architecturally warranted (the real defect: `fixStaleCloseVisibility` AC6 asserted `adv_change_archive` `target_path` routing — a surface that does not and should not exist). Previously this was caught only by the late design-phase validator, after the criterion was already minted into the contract. This change moves the catch to an early, structural gate.

## What Was Built

- **Mint-time structural warrant verification.** Capability-presuming criteria declare a typed `[warrant: tool:<name>#<arg> | spec:<rq-id>]` tag in the agreement. `contract-mint` parses, strips, records, and verifies each declared warrant against a live tool-surface + spec-id lookup; an unresolved warrant fails the mint with `CONTRACT_UNRESOLVED_WARRANT`. (`validator/warrant.ts`, `validator/contract-mint.ts`)
- **Live, cycle-free tool surface.** `tool-registry.getToolSurface()` exposes tool name → arg-key sets, injected into the pure validator via a runtime dynamic import in `adv_contract_mint` — no generated artifact, no import cycle, no drift. (DDC2 guarded by an import-boundary test.)
- **Discovery process layer.** `adv-discover` now requires every reproduction-sourced finding to be classified `broken_capability | unwarranted_operation | unverified`, forbids `unwarranted_operation`/`unverified` findings from seeding a must-work criterion, and requires a warrant declaration for capability-presuming criteria.
- **Spec law.** `rq-acWarrant01` added to `advance-workflow` (spec + docs mirror).
- **Backfill.** `fixStaleCloseVisibility` AC6 corrected (status-repair cross-project routing only; archive has no `target_path` by design) and re-minted.
- **Durable wisdom** capturing the rule, promoted to project level.

## Honest Boundary

Structure verifies *declared* warrants (the AC6-class failure: declared-but-wrong). It does not infer an *undeclared* capability-presuming criterion from prose — that would be the heuristic-authority anti-pattern this change removes (C5/DONT4). The discovery classification layer forces the declaration; the mint check makes it truthful. The design validator remains as a backstop.

## Verification

- Independent adv-reviewer acceptance review: **READY**, 0 findings.
- Contract review matrix: 19/19 rows pass/respected, 0 failing.
- `pnpm run check` green (schemas, typecheck, isolation, lockfile, lint, format).
- Affected suites green: warrant, contract-mint, warrant-boundary, tool-registry.surface, ac-warrant-guard-assets, spec-citation-invariant, discover-asset suites.
- Campsite-fixed two pre-existing trunk failures: cited `rq-changeLifecycleState01`; bumped `advance-workflow` 1.22.0 → 1.23.0 (this change's added requirement) and aligned a stale version pin.

## Deferred / Follow-up

- Live end-to-end validation of warrant verification through the deployed `adv_contract_mint` requires a plugin rebuild + OpenCode restart (source-vs-dist gotcha); validated in-session via unit/integration tests.
documents.acceptance:
# Acceptance

Reviewed at: 2026-06-25T14:52:36.219Z

## Contract Review Matrix

| ID | Kind | Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SC1 | success_criterion | **SC1:** An acceptance criterion that presumes a capability surface which does not exist cannot reach planning — it is rejected at or before contract mint with a precise, actionable error. | pass | Catch moved to mint: contract-mint.ts throws CONTRACT_UNRESOLVED_WARRANT before persistence; tool-registry.surface.test.ts proves live-surface rejection of a nonexistent surface. |
| SC2 | success_criterion | **SC2:** A failed reproduction attempt is explicitly distinguishable from a real requirement in discovery output, so it cannot silently seed a criterion asserting the capability must work. | pass | adv-discover.md 'Reproduction Finding Classification' section + rq-acWarrant01 require broken_capability|unwarranted_operation|unverified tagging; ac-warrant-guard-assets.test.ts asserts presence. |
| SC3 | success_criterion | **SC3:** Changes with no capability-presuming criteria gain no added ceremony (proportionality preserved). | pass | Proportionality: behavioral criteria with no warrant tag mint unchanged (contract-mint.test.ts AC3 test). |
| AC1 | acceptance_criterion | **AC1:** Given an agreement that declares a capability warrant naming a tool surface absent from the tool registry, when the contract is minted, then mint fails fast with a precise error identifying the unresolved warrant. | pass | contract-mint.test.ts AC1 + tool-registry.surface.test.ts: tool:adv_change_archive#target_path → CONTRACT_UNRESOLVED_WARRANT fail-fast. |
| AC2 | acceptance_criterion | **AC2:** Given an agreement that declares a capability warrant naming a real tool surface, when the contract is minted, then mint succeeds. | pass | contract-mint.test.ts AC2 + surface test: tool:adv_change_status_repair#target_path resolves; item.warrants recorded; [warrant:] tag stripped. |
| AC3 | acceptance_criterion | **AC3:** Given an agreement whose criteria declare no capability warrants (purely behavioral), when the contract is minted, then mint succeeds unchanged. | pass | contract-mint.test.ts: behavioral criteria with no warrant tag mint unchanged (no lookup needed). |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | **AC4:** The `adv-discover` command contract requires every reproduction-sourced finding to be classified as broken_capability | unwarranted_operation | unverified, and forbids an unwarranted_operation or unverified finding from seeding a criterion asserting the capability must work. | pass | ac-warrant-guard-assets.test.ts: adv-discover.md classification + forbid-unwarranted-seeding + warrant-declaration assertions pass (6/6). |
| AC5 | acceptance_criterion | **AC5:** The `advance-workflow` spec contains a normative requirement (rq-*) for capability-warrant validation at contract mint and reproduction-finding classification at discovery. | pass | ac-warrant-guard-assets.test.ts: advance-workflow spec.json contains rq-acWarrant01 with body + scenarios (incl. CONTRACT_UNRESOLVED_WARRANT, unwarranted_operation). |
| AC6 | acceptance_criterion | **AC6:** `fixStaleCloseVisibility` AC6 is corrected to reflect status-repair cross-project routing only, with archive's no-target_path-by-design rationale recorded. | pass | fixStaleCloseVisibility contract.items AC6 readback confirms status-repair cross-project routing only + adv_change_archive no-target_path-by-design rationale; reviewer confirmed. |
| AC7 | acceptance_criterion | **AC7:** `pnpm run check` and the affected plugin test suites pass. | pass | pnpm run check PASS (schemas/typecheck/isolation/lockfile/lint/format); affected suites green incl. spec-citation-invariant and discover-asset suites; reviewer reran clean. |
| C1 | constraint | **C1:** Patch source in `/home/jon/dev/advance`; no deployed-artifact edits. | respected | All edits in worktree change/addAcWarrantGuard; no deployed runtime artifact edits; git status clean. |
| C2 | constraint | **C2:** The primary guard is structural (registry/spec verification at mint); prose rules are secondary, never the sole authority (P33). | respected | Warrant verified structurally via live getToolSurface() lookup, not prose heuristic (P33). |
| C3 | constraint | **C3:** The warrant requirement is proportional — only capability-presuming criteria carry warrants. | respected | Proportional: only capability-presuming criteria carry warrants; behavioral untouched (AC3). |
| C4 | constraint | **C4:** Existing specs are law; extend without contradiction. Existing agreements without declared warrants must continue to mint. | respected | Existing warrant-less agreements still mint (AC3); new rq-acWarrant01 additive, no spec contradiction. |
| C5 | constraint | **C5:** No heuristic NLP inference of "presumed capability" from free prose as the verification authority. | respected | No heuristic NLP inference of presumed capability; only declared warrants verified. |
| DONT1 | avoidance | **DONT1:** Do not treat a failed reproduction attempt as a requirement. | respected | Failed repro != requirement: classification rule + warrant-declaration enforce this. |
| DONT2 | avoidance | **DONT2:** Do not allow a hedged/unverified observation to harden into a firm criterion without a declared warrant. | respected | Hedge not hardened: unverified classification blocks seeding must-work criteria. |
| DONT3 | avoidance | **DONT3:** Do not rely solely on the design-phase validator as the catch for unwarranted criteria. | respected | Design validator not sole catch: mint-time structural check + discovery classification layers added. |
| DONT4 | avoidance | **DONT4:** Do not impose warrant ceremony on behavioral criteria that presume no capability surface. | respected | No warrant ceremony on behavioral criteria (C3 + AC3 proportionality test). |
